title: GRAM: an off-switch for an AI's most dangerous knowledge
imageStyle: iridescent neural chrome, violet and magenta holographic glow, dark research-lab atmosphere, high detail

scenes.text: The same AI that helps *cure* a disease can help *make* one
scenes.voiceSegment: The same AI knowledge that helps cure a disease can help make one. That's the dual-use problem.
scenes.backdropPrompt: dark high-tech virology laboratory, rows of glass vials glowing faintly violet, biosafety hood, moody light

scenes.leftText: The powerful model — and the misuse risk
scenes.rightText: Play it safe — and lose the good it can do
scenes.voiceSegment: Until now it was all or nothing: ship the powerful model and risk misuse, or hold it back and lose the good it can do.

scenes.imagePrompt: glowing central AI brain core made of chrome, with several small detachable glowing modules docked around it labeled by color, one clearly separable, iridescent violet and magenta, isometric
scenes.text: GRAM: each dangerous skill goes in its own removable module
scenes.voiceSegment: Researchers at A E Studio and Anthropic built a fix, called GRAM. During training, each dangerous skill, like virology or cyber, gets packed into its own small, removable module.

scenes.imagePrompt: a single glowing chrome module being pulled out of an AI core, the empty socket going dark and cold while the rest stays lit, dramatic violet rim light, isometric
scenes.text: Delete it → the model acts like it never learned it
scenes.voiceSegment: Delete that module, and the model acts like it never learned the subject at all.

scenes.text: Keeps the good, drops the danger
scenes.voiceSegment: And it keeps its normal abilities almost perfectly, while the removed capability drops away, cleaner than just filtering the data or trying to make it forget later.

scenes.label: and it only gets cleaner with scale
scenes.voiceSegment: They tested it up to five billion parameters, and it only gets better with scale. Bigger models are harder to trick back into the deleted knowledge.
scenes.backdropPrompt: vast dark data center training hall, endless rows of servers with violet and magenta indicator lights receding into the distance

scenes.imagePrompt: a large glowing physical switch or dial on a control panel, one setting labeled with a lock, chrome and iridescent violet, a hand reaching toward it, cinematic
scenes.text: An off-switch: trusted labs get the full model, everyone else the safe one
scenes.voiceSegment: It's basically an off-switch: trusted labs get the full model, everyone else gets the safe one. From a single training run, five times cheaper than training separate models.

scenes.text: A switch for what AI is allowed to *know*
scenes.voiceSegment: A switch for what an A I is allowed to know. Follow for daily AI news.
